title: Random Match Probability Product Rule Calculator
description: Apply the product rule to calculate a DNA profile's random match probability (RMP). A table of STR loci shows each locus's genotype and population frequency; toggle loci in and out with checkboxes and watch the running product shrink on a log scale, then read the RMP as a fraction, as '1 in X', and compared to U.S. and world population sizes.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
DNA Profiling
Random Match Probability
STR Loci
Population Genetics
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — calculate random match probability using the product rule across multiple STR loci.

educational.topic: Calculating random match probability with the product rule
learningObjectives: Apply the product rule by multiplying single-locus genotype frequencies to obtain a multi-locus random match probability.
Express a random match probability in both scientific-notation and '1 in X' form.
Explain how adding loci drives the probability exponentially smaller and compare the result to real population sizes.
educational.prerequisites: Idea that an STR locus has a genotype with a population frequency; multiplying decimals/probabilities; reading scientific notation

pedagogical.interactionPattern: A table lists eight STR loci with realistic genotype frequencies, each with a checkbox. As the student toggles loci on and off, a running-product curve on a log scale updates to show the cumulative probability dropping locus by locus. 'Calculate Product' reveals the final RMP in scientific notation and as '1 in X', and a 'How rare is that?' panel draws log-scaled bars comparing the inverse RMP to U.S. and world population sizes. 'Select All' and 'Clear' set the locus set quickly.
pedagogical.assessment: Active calculation: the student builds the product themselves and sees the statistical power accumulate, reaching the 'wow moment' when the match probability falls below the world population. The population comparison gives a concrete check on whether the profile is rare enough to be individualizing.
pedagogical.modelType: Product-rule model: RMP is the cumulative product of the included loci's genotype frequencies; the running product and a log-scale curve are recomputed live, and the inverse RMP is compared against fixed population reference values.
controls: One checkbox per STR locus (include/exclude from the product)
Calculate Product button (reveal the RMP and population comparison)
